repository.description: [READ ONLY] A sandbox to visualize Jetpack components in isolation. This repository is the published storybook, for issue tracking and development head to: https://github.com/automattic/jetpack

commit.message:
docs: enrich README news and section descriptions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rem: close challenger criticals — full header auth + atomic write + event/flag validation

- SHA-256 now covers zeroed-header+schema+spirv+payload (was schema+spirv+payload only)
  Closes header metadata tamper gap: event_count, flags, and length fields are now authenticated
- compile(): atomic write via tmp-pid file + rename (no truncated stones on crash/kill)
- query(): validate_flags() gate before any block extraction; rejects unknown bits,
  conflicting CPU+GPU, and unimplemented GPU path
- query(): validate() called on every decoded TrailEvent after decode
- Added test_header_tamper_detection: flips event_count byte, must fail SHA-256 check
- 18/18 tests pass (was 17/17 + new regression test)

Challenger (GPT-5.4): found 7 criticals; critical #1 pre-fixed by architect StoneEvent wrapper,
criticals #2-4 addressed here. Criticals #5-7 (memory bounds, race/lock, BOM scope) deferred
to documented Phase 2 constraints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Step 9 Part 3: DevConsole & Normalized Dev Event Model (#49)

* feat(dev-tools): add DevConsole abstraction with structured event model

Implement the real DevConsole for normalized development-time logging:

- Add DevConsole types: DevConsoleLevel, DevConsoleOptions, BuildSuccessInfo,
  ServerStatusInfo, DevConsole interface to dev-types.ts
- Implement createDevConsole factory with ANSI color support, log level
  filtering, timestamp toggling, clear behavior, and formatted output
- Add connectToDevServer method for automatic event routing from DevServer
- Wire dev-command.ts to use DevConsole instead of scattered console.info calls
- Update barrel exports across dev-tools/index, build/index, and src/index
- Add 32 unit tests covering all Suite 4 test spec requirements (DC-39 to DC-64)
- Update DC-03 test to verify stdout-based DevConsole output

commit.message:
Implement Orphek integration with enhanced device discovery and setup (#1)

* feat: implement Orphek integration with support for OR4-iCon LED Bar

- Add GitHub Actions workflows for pull requests and releases
- Create configuration flow for device setup
- Implement API client for device communication
- Update coordinator and light platform to manage device state
- Enhance documentation and troubleshooting guide
- Update manifest and translation files for new features

* working with tuya app

* Refactor Orphek integration to support Orphek account login and improve device discovery

- Updated config flow to allow login via Orphek account (email/password) instead of Tuya Cloud credentials.
- Implemented new schemas for user input and device discovery.
- Enhanced device discovery to include both LAN and cloud devices, handling local keys more effectively.
- Added new sensor entities for temperature, mode, and schedule information.
- Improved error handling and user feedback during setup.
- Updated documentation strings and translations to reflect changes in setup process.
- Added support for additional features in the light entity, including effects.

* refactor: streamline Orphek login flow and remove unused Tuya integration

feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool (#4609)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool

Implements roadmap item #219 (v1.9.0):

- Remove per-invocation cudaMalloc/cudaFree from launchHnswSearchKernel();
  caller now provides pre-allocated d_visited pool (nullptr → overflow signal)
- Add persistent visited bitset pool to CudaHnswTraversalEngine::Impl:
  allocated once in buildIndex() at maxBatchSize × ceil(numNodes/8) bytes,
  freed in freeDevice()
- Implement chunked batch processing in batchSearch(): when pool_capacity <
  num_queries, split into sub-batches of pool_capacity, process serially,
  concatenate on host. Covers both single-pass and multi-pass (large-k) paths.
- Expose CudaHnswTraversalEngine::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n) and
  CUDAVectorBackend::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n)
- Clamp pool to 90% of BackendCapabilities::maxMemoryBytes in buildHnswAnnIndex()
- Set BackendHealthStatus::makeDegraded() via setError() when pool allocation
  fails so getHealthStatus() surfaces the issue to callers
- Add 14 new tests: setMaxBatchSize, hasVisitedPool, chunked correctness,
  degraded health on pool failure
- Update FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md and ROADMAP.md to mark item #219 done

commit.message:
Make HTTPS the default; rename flags for clarity

- HTTPS is now the default upstream scheme (no flag needed)
- -insecure: use plain HTTP to upstream (previously required -ssl for https)
- -skip-verify: skip TLS cert verification for self-signed certs
  (previously -insecure)
- -ssl: removed
- Updated usage text, README examples, and log messages

commit.message:
fix(tui): hierarchy navigation uses correct parent for children query

The task selector was using session mapping (Feature ID) instead of
current_parent_work_item (User Story ID) when drilling into children.
This caused sub-level items like Bug #274141 to not appear.

Key changes:
- Prioritize current_parent_work_item over session mapping when in sub-levels
- Add parent_stack for multi-level back navigation (Backspace/Ctrl+O)
- Show breadcrumb in title when viewing sub-level children
- Smart Backspace: pop hierarchy when filter empty, back to features at top
- Fix cache key to also use current_parent_work_item

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

PR Reviewer: auto-fix staging, auto-generate, history restore

Git-style auto-fix workflow:
- 'Fix' button generates a code fix via Copilot CLI and stages it
- Staged fixes panel shows colored diff previews
- 'Unstage' removes a fix, 'Commit All Fixes' pushes to PR branch
- Fix buttons only appear on your own PRs (author detection via ADO API)

Auto-generate review:
- Loading a PR now automatically generates AI review (no extra click)

History restore:
- 'Restore' button on history entries reloads the PR with saved comments
- Restored reviews have full Post/Fix/Delete capabilities

ADO client additions:
- get_current_user() for author detection
- get_file_at_branch() for fetching source files
- get_branch_head() + push_file_changes() for committing fixes
- author_id stored in pr_info

AI reviewer additions:
- generate_fix() uses Copilot CLI to produce fixed file content
- Strips markdown fences from fix output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address PR #5 review comments

Code fixes:
- Server: graceful degradation on FC dir creation failure (no panic)
- Sandbox IDs: use Uuid::simple() for consistent fc- prefix format
- Machine config: wire memory_mb/vcpu_count via Firecracker REST API
- Concurrency: release global write lock before async operations
  (start, execute, upload, download, stop no longer block other VMs)
- KVM check: is_available() now verifies /dev/kvm exists and is accessible
- Path validation: use Path::components() instead of contains("..") in executor
- Vsock port: document coupling between config and executor constant
- Integration test: increase FC timeout from 30s to 75s

Doc fixes:
- CRATE_REFERENCE: correct JSON field names (content_base64, not data)
- CRATE_REFERENCE: fix state_dir/workspace_dir default paths
- CRATE_REFERENCE: fix dependency versions (hyper 0.14, hyperlocal 0.8)
- CRATE_REFERENCE: fix vsock UDS path format
- KNOWN_ISSUES: vsock connection is persistent, not one-shot
- KNOWN_ISSUES: correct cleanup paths for stale state

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Sprint 1: Repairs.jsx Step 3 — service tier cards replacing Service Type dropdown, anchor banner (auto-taper), trust signal row; Priority Level Field wrapper fix; Step 5 — ProCare member discount preview panel
- Sprint 2: Checkout.jsx — points balance fetch (AuthContext), slider + apply/remove redemption UI, coupon injected via syncAndPlace coupon_codes param; cart.js syncAndPlace updated to apply coupons via /cart/apply-coupon; Rewards.jsx page created (/rewards) with balance, earn guide, redemption flow (slider→coupon→copy), paginated activity history; OrderConfirmation.jsx — 6 repair-specific WC statuses added, points earned banner on completed orders
- Sprint 3: membership.js API client (MEMBERSHIP_TIERS, FOUNDING_MEMBER_PROMO, calculateMemberDiscount, isFoundingMemberWindowOpen); dtb-membership.php mu-plugin (GET /tiers public, GET /status JWT, POST /enroll JWT, WC order hook for renewals); 00-dtb-loader.php updated; ProMembership.jsx page (/pro-membership) with tier cards, founding member promo, warranty comparison
- Sprint 4: orders.js getCustomerOrders(); dtb-rest-api.php GET /drywall/v1/orders?customer= route (customer param required, sanitized); Orders.jsx page (/orders) with paginated order list + status badges; Dashboard.jsx — StatCard/SideNavLink icon prop bug fixed, useState/useEffect data fetching for points+membership, ProCare status card, sidebar nav updated; AccountSettings.jsx stub (/account-settings); App.jsx — Orders, Rewards, ProMembership, AccountSettings lazy imports and routes added

commit.message:
feat(cli): custom multiline editor — no backslash, no continuation prefix

Replace readline-based prompt() with standalone readMultilineInput():
- ANSI escape rendering: clear-and-redraw input area on every edit
- Clean aligned continuation lines (spaces, not '...' prefix)
- No visible backslash at line ends
- Live in-place editing: backspace across lines redraws properly
- Ctrl+C clears input (with text) or exits (when empty)
- Ctrl+D deletes char or exits on empty
- All editing shortcuts: Ctrl+A/E/U/K/W, arrow keys, Home/End
- Bracketed paste with native multiline support
- Shift+Enter, Alt+Enter, Ctrl+J for newlines
- Tab completion for /commands preserved
- readline kept only for session picker, then closed

commit.message:
Fixes #924 - Add enable_hard_delete feature flag for bypassing workspace recycle bin (#926)

With the Fabric workspace recycle bin, soft-deleted items still count as
active dependents, breaking cascading deletes (e.g., pipeline + notebook
both removed from source control — pipeline soft-deletes fine, but
notebook deletion fails because the recycled pipeline still holds a
reference).

Adds an opt-in `enable_hard_delete` feature flag that appends
`?hardDelete=true` to the DELETE API call, permanently bypassing the
recycle bin. This is opt-in because hard delete requires **workspace
Admin role** vs. write permissions for soft delete.

## Changes

- **`constants.py`** — New `FeatureFlag.ENABLE_HARD_DELETE =
"enable_hard_delete"` enum member
- **`fabric_workspace.py`** — `_unpublish_item` conditionally appends
`?hardDelete=true` when flag is set
- **`optional_feature.md`** — Documents the new flag and Admin role
requirement
- **`tests/test_hard_delete.py`** — Tests for URL with/without flag, and
via `append_feature_flag`

## Usage

```python
from fabric_cicd import append_feature_flag
append_feature_flag("enable_hard_delete")
```

Or via config YAML:
```yaml
features:
  - enable_hard_delete
```

## Linked Issue (REQUIRED)

> [!WARNING]
>
> <details>
> <summary>Firewall rules blocked me from connecting to one or more
addresses (expand for details)</summary>
>
> #### I tried to connect to the following addresses, but was blocked by
firewall rules:
>
> - `releases.astral.sh`
> - Triggering command: `/home/REDACTED/.local/bin/uv uv sync --dev -q`
(dns block)
>
> If you need me to access, download, or install something from one of
these locations, you can either:
>
> - Configure [Actions setup
steps](https://gh.io/copilot/actions-setup-steps) to set up my
environment, which run before the firewall is enabled
> - Add the appropriate URLs or hosts to the custom allowlist in this
repository's [Copilot coding agent
settings](https://github.com/microsoft/fabric-cicd/settings/copilot/coding_agent)
(admins only)
>
> </details>

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


## Problem

With the introduction of [Workspace recycle bin
(preview)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/workspaces#workspace-ycle-bin-preview),
deleted items are now moved to the recycle bin instead of being
permanently deleted. This breaks deployments when deleting items that
have dependencies (e.g., a pipeline depending on a notebook).

When both a pipeline and its dependent notebook are removed from source
control, the deployment:
1. "Deletes" the pipeline (soft-deletes it to recycle bin)
2. Tries to delete the notebook, but **fails** because the pipeline in
the recycle bin still counts as an active dependent

See issue: https://github.com/microsoft/fabric-cicd/issues/924

## Solution

Add a new `enable_hard_delete` feature flag that appends
`?hardDelete=true` to the DELETE API call when unpublishing items. This
uses the [Items - Delete Item API's `hardDelete` query
parameter](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/fabric/core/items/delete-item)
to permanently delete items, bypassing the recycle bin.

**Important**: Hard delete requires the **workspace Admin role** (vs.
write permissions for soft delete), so this must be an opt-in feature
flag, not a hardcoded default.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Add the feature flag to `FeatureFlag` enum in
`src/fabric_cicd/constants.py`

Add a new enum member:
```python
ENABLE_HARD_DELETE = "enable_hard_delete"
"""Set to enable hard deletion of items, bypassing the workspace recycle bin."""
```

### 2. Update `_unpublish_item` in `src/fabric_cicd/fabric_workspace.py`

In the `_unpublish_item` method (around line 789), modify the DELETE URL
to conditionally include the `hardDelete=true` query parameter when the
feature flag is enabled:

```python
# Current code (line 789):
api_response = self.endpoint.invoke(method="DELETE", url=f"{self.base_api_url}/items/{item_guid}")

# Should become:
hard_delete = FeatureFlag.ENABLE_HARD_DELETE.value in constants.FEATURE_FLAG
delete_url = f"{self.base_api_url}/items/{item_guid}" + ("?hardDelete=true" if hard_delete else "")
api_response = self.endpoint.invoke(method="DELETE", url=delete_url)
```

### 3. Update documentation in `docs/how_to/optional_feature.md`

Add a new row to the feature flags table:

| Flag Name | Description | Experimental |
|---|---|---|
| `enable_hard_delete` | Set to enable hard deletion of items, bypassing
the workspace recycle bin. Requires workspace Admin role. | |

### 4. Add tests

Add tests to verify:
- When `enable_hard_delete` flag is set, the DELETE URL includes
`?hardDelete=true`
- When the flag is NOT set, the DELETE URL does NOT include the
`hardDelete` parameter
- The feature flag works correctly via both
`append_feature_flag("enable_hard_delete")` and config YAML `features`
section

Follow the existing testing patterns in the `tests/` directory. Mock
external API calls using `requests_mock`. Make sure to clear feature
flags between tests to avoid state leakage.


The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat
exploration (may be truncated):

User: Should we hardcode harddelete=True to retain the same behavior
before the API change, introduce a new feature flag for users to toggle
the delete type

Assistant: 

Great question. Let me analyze the codebase to understand the current
delete implementation, the feature flag system, and how best to approach
this.

Assistant: Great question. Now I have a clear picture of the codebase
and the API behavior. Let me summarize the analysis:

## Recommendation: **Introduce a new feature flag**
(`enable_hard_delete`) rather than hardcoding `hardDelete=true`

Here's the reasoning:

### Why not hardcode `hardDelete=true`?

1. **Breaking change in permissions**: The [Delete Item
API](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/fabric/core/items/delete-item)
requires the **workspace Admin role** for hard deletes, but only **write
permissions** for soft deletes. Hardcoding `hardDelete=true` would break
deployments for users running with write-only credentials.

2. **Intentional API behavior**: Microsoft introduced the recycle bin
and soft delete as a safety feature. Some users may *want* the
recoverability — forcing hard delete for everyone takes that choice
away.

3. **Existing pattern in the codebase**: The project already has a
well-established feature flag pattern for destructive operations. Look
at the `UNPUBLISH_FLAG_MAPPING` in `constants.py` — Lakehouses,
Warehouses, SQL Databases, Eventhouses, and KQL Databases all require
explicit opt-in flags before deletion. Hard delete is a similarly
destructive escalation and fits this same pattern perfectly.

### Proposed implementation

**1. Add the feature flag to `FeatureFlag` enum** in
`src/fabric_cicd/constants.py`:

```python name=src/fabric_cicd/constants.py url=https://github.com/microsoft/fabric-cicd/blob/60d0e678448e087f6ce188c92b879284ec519cad/src/fabric_cicd/constants.py#L105-L140
ENABLE_HARD_DELETE = "enable_hard_delete"
"""Set to enable hard deleti...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>

commit.message:
Initial release of maskproxy

A transparent rewriting reverse proxy in Go that masks an upstream site
from clients by bidirectionally rewriting hostnames and user-defined
string pairs.

Features:
- Target host + all subdomains masked behind proxy address by default
- -exact-domain flag for exact-host-only masking
- Bidirectional string replacement (-replace original:alias,...)
- Case-insensitive replacement (-ci)
- Subdomain routing via /__sd__/ prefix preserving original host
- gzip decompression before body rewrite
- CSP / HSTS / key-pinning headers stripped
- Set-Cookie domain rewrite
- External URL protection (third-party CDN links preserved)
- Body size limit (50 MiB) to prevent OOM
- TLS skip-verify support (-insecure)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: 用 node:22-alpine 重新生成 package-lock.json 修复 Docker npm ci 失败

本地 npm 11 生成的 lockfile 与 Docker node:22-alpine (npm 10) 不兼容，
导致 'Missing: @emnapi/core@1.9.2 from lock file' 错误。
使用 node:22-alpine 容器重新生成 lockfile 解决。

commit.message:
fix(agent-hub): eliminate duplicate message on stream completion

streaming.feedEvent(completedEvent) flushes the scheduler
asynchronously, so isStreaming stays true briefly after finalize()
adds the static message — both render simultaneously causing a
visible duplicate. Use streaming.reset() instead for synchronous
cleanup since the full text is already added as a static message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
tui: implement /agents overlay and restore text selection

/agents: wire up TUI handler that formats agent definitions and live
status into a scrollable info overlay (list, info, status, create,
delete, help subcommands all supported).

Mouse capture: remove EnableMouseCapture — it prevents native terminal
text selection (copy-paste). Keyboard scroll (PageUp/PageDown and
Shift+Up/Down) remains. Mouse scroll sacrificed for text selection
which is more important for daily use.

commit.message:
feat: 添加前端测试框架和65个组件/Store测试

- 修复 GameControlButton TS2367 编译错误（gameActive 判断提前到 lobby 分支前）
- 安装 vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom
- 配置 vite.config.ts 的 test 环境（jsdom + CSS modules）
- 新增 5 个测试文件，共 65 个测试：
  - gameStore: 24 tests（所有 WS 消息处理、状态管理、事务日志）
  - GameControlButton: 12 tests（各阶段按钮显示、API 调用）
  - PlayerCard: 13 tests（徽章显示、状态渲染）
  - PotDisplay: 9 tests（阶段标签、底池显示）
  - SettlePanel: 7 tests（分配逻辑、API 调用）
- CI workflow 拆分为 backend-tests + frontend-tests 两个 job

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
